approach: REVISE unit/lifecycle nudge: make it robust to the harness boot race so a successful nudge means the peer actually took the turn, not merely staged text. Additive to the frozen lifecycle.feature (existing nudge scenarios unchanged -> self-clears, stays @frozen, no re-open).

Chose submit-then-verify-then-retry (issue option b) over verify-readiness-before-submit (option a): adapter-general + harness-agnostic verify anchor (the caller's own staged text), fails loud on cap exhaustion. Rationale in 150-nudge-boot-race.design.md.

impl: adapter gains a bare-Enter submit primitive (herdr pane run <id> "" / tmux send-keys Enter) distinct from send's text+Enter; a session-layer nudge helper sends once, reads back, resubmits the staged buffer up to a bounded cap, throws on exhaustion. Bind the new scenarios with mocked-exec tests (prior nudge scenarios landed UNBOUND).

Low-medium blast: adapter seam + one new verb-helper + tests; no registry/worktree path touched. Reversible feature branch; hard floors still stop. Sibling cr128 also adds additive scenarios to lifecycle.feature -> mail operator on hard overlap.

why: Cold sdd-spec-judge 3-lens over the 4 additive nudge boot-race scenarios: oracle/builder/architect all PASS, ALIGNED true, no blocker, no open markers. gherkin-cli diff addOnly:true (4 added / 0 modified / 0 removed) -> lifecycle.feature stays @frozen, self-clears, no re-open. Existing 22 scenarios untouched. Scenarios cover verify-without-needless-retry (happy path), re-submit on boot-race swallow (the fix), no message duplication on flush, and fail-loud on cap exhaustion (no false success). Each Then boolean/observable; check-suite OK, check-spec-state OK. README nudge bullet + table synced to the suite. Non-blocking cosmetic: new scenarios assert on 'nudge' (the command) vs old 'the session adapter' (mechanism) since the verify+retry lives above the adapter; kept intentionally. Self-asserted within auto-all leash (additive, no hard floor).
